name: Lunar Prospector
fact: Direct neutron-spectrometer detection of hydrogen at both lunar poles — strong evidence for water ice in permanently-shadowed crater floors. Deliberately impacted into Shoemaker crater at end of mission, hoping the impact ejecta would confirm water; Earth-based observers detected nothing definitive (a follow-up only delivered by LCROSS in 2009).
capability: Confirmed Clementine's polar-ice hypothesis; established neutron-spectroscopy as the primary tool for lunar volatiles.
